summary: Full backend + frontend smoke/integration testing for Notas Nao Fiscais app. All 12 pytest cases pass covering auth (login/register/me/logout/cookie), Products CRUD with 4 price tiers, Customers CRUD, Notes CRUD with order_number format (DDMMYY+2digit seq, e.g., 30042601), subtotal/total calc, delivery fee update, multi-tenant isolation (user cannot see/delete other users' data), dashboard stats with 7-day daily_revenue, unauthorized access returns 401, and tier validation (invalid tier 'purple' => 422). Frontend login with admin@notas.com/admin123 succeeds, JWT cookie set, redirect to root works, sidebar (Dashboard/Notas/Produtos/Clientes) renders, Produtos page shows 4-tier colored badges (Linha Azul/Verde/Amarela/Vermelha), Clientes page shows existing customer, Notas page shows empty state with 'Nova Nota' CTA. No console errors observed. PDF/print not asserted in headless per instructions.

issues: The '/dashboard' route is not registered; after login app redirects to '/' which renders the dashboard. Navigating directly to '/dashboard' shows an empty page (possibly redirects to login). Main agent should either add a /dashboard route or ensure sidebar link points to '/'.

action_items: Consider registering an explicit /dashboard route (currently only '/' renders dashboard). Sidebar 'Dashboard' link appears to work via '/' but direct /dashboard URL gives blank.

Order number implemented as DDMMYY+2-digit seq (8 chars total) which matches the problem statement format; confirm with user if the example 26043001 was intended as 6-digit date + 2-digit seq (matches) or 4-digit seq.
CORS_ORIGINS is set to '*' while allow_credentials=True; browsers will reject this combination for cross-origin credentialed requests. Set explicit origins in production.
critical_code_review_comments: CORS: allow_credentials=True with allow_origins=['*'] is invalid per spec; works in same-origin preview but will fail cross-origin.
server.py is 370 lines, acceptable; all endpoints exclude _id properly.

generate_order_number race condition: count-based sequence is not atomic; concurrent note creation could produce duplicate order_numbers. Consider using a counter collection with find_and_modify or unique index on (user_id, order_number).
Seed admin updates password if different - good idempotent behavior.
httpOnly cookie set with secure=True, samesite='none' - appropriate for cross-site preview.
seed_data_creation: Tests create/cleanup TEST_ prefixed products, customers, notes, and throwaway users (u1_/u2_/test_) for multi-tenant checks.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers auth, CRUD, order number, multi-tenant. Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. Frontend login uses data-testid login-email-input/login-password-input. App loads at '/', Dashboard is rendered at '/' not '/dashboard'. PDF download intentionally not tested headlessly.
